where: Different payment processing algorithms (credit card, PayPal, UPI, crypto)
why: Payment methods are added/removed frequently. Each has completely different processing logic. Client (user) chooses at checkout.

where: Pricing calculation varies by vehicle type, time, and subscription
why: Parking lots constantly change pricing rules. Peak hours, weekends, subscriptions -- each is a separate algorithm. New pricing = new class.

where: Fare calculation differs by ride type
why: Fare algorithms change independently. Pool has shared-ride discounts, Premium has base fees. Surge wraps any fare with a multiplier (Strategy + Decorator).

where: Discount calculation varies by customer type and promotions
why: Promotions change weekly. VIP gets 20% off, employees get flat $50, festivals get BOGO. Each is a separate algorithm chosen at checkout.

where: Scheduling algorithm decides which elevator serves which request
why: Buildings have different needs. A hospital needs nearest-first. An office tower needs zone-based. A mall needs load-balanced. Same controller, different algorithm.

where: Eviction policy decides which entry to remove when cache is full
why: Different use cases need different eviction. Session cache needs LRU, CDN needs LFU, message queue needs FIFO. The cache itself doesn't change.

where: Delivery channel varies (email, SMS, push, Slack)
why: Users choose their preferred channel. New channels (WhatsApp, Teams) are added without touching existing ones. Client preference drives strategy selection.

where: Compression algorithm varies (ZIP, GZIP, RAR, LZ4)
why: Different files need different compression. Text compresses well with GZIP, images need LZ4 for speed. The compressor is the same, the algorithm swaps.

where: Auth method varies (password, OAuth, SSO, biometric)
why: Apps support multiple login methods. Enterprise adds SSO, mobile adds biometric. Each auth flow is completely different. Auth manager delegates to the strategy.

where: Sorting algorithm is swappable (bubble, merge, quick, heap)
why: The visualizer UI stays the same. User picks the algorithm. Each sort has different time complexity and behavior. Perfect Strategy use case -- the textbook example.
